title: Fix example in `populate_by_name` documentation
body:
<!-- Thank you for your contribution! -->
<!-- Unless your change is trivial, please create an issue to discuss the change before creating a PR -->

## Change Summary

fix the docstring of ConfigDict's populate_by_name.

## Related issue number

fix #11765 

- #11765 

<!-- WARNING: please use "fix #123" style references so the issue is closed when this PR is merged. -->

## Checklist

* [ ] The pull request title is a good summary of the changes - it will be used in the changelog
* [x] Unit tests for the changes exist
* [x] Tests pass on CI
* [x] Documentation reflects the changes where applicable
* [ ] My PR is ready to review, **please add a comment including the phrase "please review" to assign reviewers**

step_plan.summary: Fix the populate_by_name example in ConfigDict's docstring in pydantic/config.py by changing the second Model instantiation to pass the attribute name (my_field='foo'), making the example match the explanatory comments.

steps.operation: Edit the populate_by_name example in the ConfigDict docstring to use the attribute name for the second Model instantiation
steps.reason: The docstring example currently passes the alias (my_alias='foo') for both instantiations, which contradicts the explanatory text stating the second instantiation demonstrates population by attribute name. Changing the second call to my_field='foo' makes the example consistent with the narrative and easier to understand.
steps.side_effects: Documentation-only change: no runtime or API behavior changes. Potential impacts: if docstrings are run as doctests or included in generated docs, those builds/tests should be run to ensure the example still executes/formatting is preserved. No code dependents or unit tests should be affected, but CI doc builds or doctest suites may need to be checked.
